title: Anti-Sith conspiracy
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Update|[[Star Wars: Darth Vader and the Ghost Prison]]}}
{{Conjecture|legends}}
{{Event
|prev=
|conc=
|next=[[Ambush on Dargulli]]<ref name="Betrayal 3">[[Empire 3|''Empire'' 3]]</ref>
|image=[[File:BetrayalCoup.jpg]]
|name=Anti-Sith conspiracy
|conflict=
|date=[[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]<ref name="Betrayal 1">[[Empire 1|''Empire'' 1]]</ref><ref name="FF135">{{FactFile|135|BET 5-6|Imperial Betrayal}}</ref>
|place=*[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="Betrayal 3" />
*''[[Mathayus]]''<ref name="Betrayal 2">[[Empire 2|''Empire'' 2]]</ref>
*[[Dargulli/Legends|Dargulli]]<ref name="Betrayal 3" />
|outcome=Death of all conspirators but [[Gauer]]<ref name="Betrayal 3" /><ref name="Betrayal 4">[[Empire 4|''Empire'' 4]]</ref>
|keyparties=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
|participants='''Conspirators:'''
*[[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Trachta]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
*Grand Moff [[Bartam]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
*[[Gauer]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
*[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kadir (Moff)|Kadir]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
*[[General/Legends|General]] [[Skosef]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
*Second Officer [[Dezsetes]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
*[[Carsan]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
'''Targets:'''
*[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
*[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]<ref name="Betrayal 1" />
}}
{{Quote|The imperative's on us. The universe can't be ruled by a theocracy of two.|Grand Moff Trachta|<ref name="Betrayal 1" />}}
The anti-[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] '''conspiracy''' was a plot carried out by several high-ranking members of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. Led by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Trachta]], the conspiracy attempted to overthrow the Sith leaders of the Empire—[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

The attempt hinged on a special detachment of [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], programmed to obey not Palpatine but Trachta. After splitting up Vader and Palpatine, the conspirators would have their stormtroopers, planted within the ranks of the two [[Gender/Legends|men's]] forces, turn on the Sith and [[Death/Legends|kill]] them.

The plot did not go as planned. The members first turned on each other, resulting in the [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] of most of the participants. The surviving members were no more successful, as Vader's and Palpatine's mastery of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] allowed them to easily defeat the stormtroopers and dispose of the remaining leaders. In the end, all members of the conspiracy but the Imperial assassin [[Gauer]] were killed in the attempt.

==The plot==
===Earlier coup===
{{Main|Ghost Prison incident}}
In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] rose to power, [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was the victim of an attempted military coup by the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] [[Headmaster/Legends|headmaster]], [[Gentis]]. [[Trachta]], a veteran of the recent [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and a [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], aided Palpatine, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], and Lieutenant [[Laurita Tohm]] in quelling the coup. In the wake of the incident, Trachta was promoted to [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]], and took over Gentis's former position of headmaster.<ref name="DV&GP">''[[Star Wars: Darth Vader and the Ghost Prison]]''</ref>

===Inception===
[[File:Betrayal_Conspiracy.jpg|thumb|left|190px|The conspirators in the coup plot]]
Trachta, who had known [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] since approximately [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]], first conceived the plan to topple him some [[Time/Legends|time]] before the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[11 BBY/Legends|11 BBY]].<ref name="Betrayal 1" /> Officially, the reason was that he despised the theocratic rule of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and wanted secular leadership for the Empire; truthfully, he sought power for himself.<ref name="Fact File">[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 122|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 122]]</ref>

In 11 BBY, Trachta first set his plan into motion by arranging for the creation of a special detachment of [[clone stormtrooper]]s, one hundred [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] with absolute loyalty to Trachta and none to Palpatine. Over the next ten years, he brought several other Imperials into his plan, including Grand Moff [[Bartam]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Skosef]], and the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Gauer]].<ref name="Betrayal 1" />

===The plan begins===
{{Quote|You've never been involved in anything like this, Gauer—well, no one's been into anything this big before.|Grand Moff Bartam|<ref name="Betrayal 1" />}}
[[File:Betrayal_Clones.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Carsan presents Trachta's clone stormtroopers.]]
By [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], the army was complete. [[Carsan]], the Imperial responsible for training the clones, took Trachta to see them and hand them over. To test their loyalty and remove a witness, Trachta ordered them to seize and [[Death/Legends|execute]] Carsan. They demonstrated their complete loyalty by doing so. Having heard that Palpatine planned to disband the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]], the conspirators decided that they would cause an even greater shift in power. By removing the Sith, whom they saw as a cult, they intended to give non-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]]—namely themselves—a chance at holding power.<ref name="Betrayal 1" />

Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], Trachta recruited two other members, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kadir (Moff)|Kadir]] of the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]] and [[Second officer/Legends|Second Officer]] [[Dezsetes]]. Kadir was upset at the loss of a new batch of stormtroopers, originally intended to augment his forces, to Grand Moff [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], and was approached by Trachta. The Grand Moff felt him out for his willingness to find a "solution," as Trachta put it. The young man proved trustworthy, and he was invited to attend a meeting of the conspirators.<ref name="Betrayal 1" /> Dezsetes, meanwhile, was planted aboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Mathayus]]'', one of the most powerful [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].<ref name="Fact File" /> Hearing from him that Vader was to travel to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dargulli/Legends|Dargulli]] on ''Mathayus'' to deal with a [[Dargulli Dark Jedi|renegade Jedi]], Trachta decided that with the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] split up, the time had come to make their move. In the twelve [[Standard day/Legends|days]] or fewer that Vader was to be gone, Trachta intended to have both him and Palpatine eliminated.<ref name="Betrayal 1" />

===The first attacks===
{{Quote|He'd never walk into a trap. Our plan depends on that.|Trachta, on Palpatine|<ref name="Betrayal 2" />}}
[[File:Betrayal_Awesome.jpg|thumb|left|190px|Darth Vader escapes the Rebel trap.]]
Some of Trachta's [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] were sent with Vader, to be commanded by Dezsetes and intended to kill the Sith. Dezsetes planned to wait until Dargulli to make his move, where the soldiers would be assigned to escort Vader. However, circumstances arose that gave the conspirator a chance to carry out his mission sooner than expected.<ref name="Fact File" />

En route to Dargulli, ''Mathayus'' intercepted a Rebel transmission about a rendezvous at [[Yorn Skot]]. Arriving in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] above the world, they detected a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] vessel emitting a distress signal.<ref name="Betrayal 1" /> There, the conspiracy had a stroke of luck: due to the fact that the original communication had been on an open channel, Dezsetes recognized that it was a trap, but let Vader walk into it anyway.<ref name="Fact File" /> ''Mathayus'' pulled the Rebel ship into a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and sent a boarding party to force its way in as Vader watched from an observation area on the hangar's side. The ship was empty, and Vader inquired as to how the original transmission had been received. When he was told, he quickly ordered the tractor beam to be released. It was almost too late, as the ship detonated in a massive explosion that shattered the windows of Vader's observation area, sending the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] flying into the rapidly decompressing hangar. Nevertheless, he recovered and made his way back to the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. Vader blamed himself for not noticing the trap, and Dezsetes's role was never detected.<ref name="Betrayal 2" />

[[File:Betrayal_BAM.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Palpatine's shuttle is destroyed.]]

As that was taking place, the first strike on Palpatine was being carried out on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The Emperor planned to visit the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], which was nearing completion. Before he left, a small number of Trachta's stormtroopers under the command of Skosef were sent on board his [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] carrying an unsuspicious crate of bottles, and secretly, a [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]]. Palpatine, however, sensed a danger through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and called a halt to his procession, which included Trachta. Moments later, the bomb detonated, killing many of Palpatine's stormtroopers and half his [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]].<ref name="Betrayal 1" />

That had all been intended by Trachta, however. Knowing that Palpatine would never walk into a trap, he had arranged for a two-part plan. The first part, though not harming Palpatine, had removed many of his protectors. To fill the gap, Trachta offered his own stormtroopers to augment the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] guard, putting them in position to carry out the final part of the plot when the time came. With Palpatine's men and Vader out of the picture, he expected the Emperor to easily fall to his own troops.<ref name="Betrayal 1" />

===Power struggles===
{{Quote|You can trust enemies to attack. You never know what allies are planning.|Trachta|<ref name="Betrayal 1" />}}
Even before they began to carry out their plan, the conspirators had already begun an internal struggle for power. Kadir had spoken with both Grand Moffs about the need for trust and unity, but both also had their own plans for retaining power. Bartam met with Gauer, suggesting to the assassin that Trachta could meet with an "accident." Trachta, becoming paranoid about Bartam taking over, mentioned to Kadir the possibility that Bartam might not live to see the end of the plot.<ref name="Betrayal 1" />

[[File:TrachtaDeath.jpg|thumb|left|190px|Trachta is assassinated by Gauer.]]

After the bombing, Trachta informed Skosef that he had been implicated, and recruited him to carry out his own plan. The General infiltrated the apartment of Bartam and executed the Grand Moff at point-blank range. Bartam's stormtrooper guard, who had been distracted, shot Skosef, killing him and inadvertently cleaning up any trace of Trachta's role in the assassination.<ref name="Betrayal 3" />

As it turned out, Trachta had misidentified the true threat to him. Moff Kadir had his own ambitions, and under his orders, Gauer then went to Trachta's apartment.<ref name="Fact File" /> Trachta was wary, though, and when the muzzle of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] poked through the crack in his apartment door, Trachta shot the unseen assailant. As he went into the hallway to investigate, however, he learned that it had only been an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]], a decoy. Gauer ambushed the Grand Moff, shooting him and ending his life.<ref name="Betrayal 3" />

===The final stages===
{{Quote|Don't get arrogant, child.|Palpatine, to Moff Kadir|<ref name="Betrayal 4" />}}
While the conspirators on Coruscant had been busy killing each other, Dezsetes had been setting up the attack on Vader. After murdering [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Coy]] for being too loyal to the Dark Lord, he attempted to send a group of the specially altered stormtroopers with Vader to the surface of Dargulli. Vader, however, wanted to go alone, and Dezsetes had no choice but to let him.<ref name="Betrayal 3" />

[[File:BetrayalElectricDeath.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Palpatine unleashes Force lightning on his attackers.]]

Now in charge of the operation, Kadir and Gauer brought their cadre of stormtroopers to the [[Emperor's throne room]]. Claiming control of Palace security, Kadir ordered the deaths of the Imperial officers who attempted to block them, and the party marched into the Throne Room. Palpatine was waiting to greet them, and simply stated "At last." Several Royal Guards then leaped out of the drapes and began killing Kadir's men. Gauer, waiting outside, heard the commotion, turned, and ran. Palpatine let the guards attack the stormtroopers for some time, then finally let loose a torrent of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], laying waste to the soldiers left standing.<ref name="Betrayal 3" />

Kadir was still alive, and made one last attempt to shoot Palpatine with a blaster. The Sith Lord, however, blocked it and blasted more lightning into the prone Moff. Though Palpatine did not approve of Kadir's arrogance in believing he could defeat the Emperor, he was impressed by the Moff's ability and supposed masterminding of the plot, and commended him for weeding out the traitors in the Empire. Kadir seized upon this, telling Palpatine that Vader had been killed, and offering himself as an apprentice. As Kadir had no Force-sensitivity, Palpatine declined, and then activated a [[Holocamera/Legends|holocam]] that showed Vader alive and well.<ref name="Betrayal 4" />

Vader, in fact, had completed his mission to Dargulli and was back on ''Mathayus'', surrounded by the altered stormtroopers and Dezsetes. He quickly eliminated the soldiers with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], then turned on Dezsetes. The officer told Vader that the Emperor was dead, and that Trachta had been behind the conspiracy. Upon hearing this, Palpatine promptly electrocuted Kadir while Vader stabbed Dezsetes. The conspiracy had been brought to an end.<ref name="Betrayal 4" />

==Principal conspirators==
===Grand Moff Trachta===
{{Main|Trachta}}
[[File:Trachta_swptp.jpg|thumb|left|130px|Grand Moff Trachta, architect of the conspiracy]]
A man encased in life-support equipment due to a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] explosion,<ref name="RECG">''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Trachta was the original architect of the plot against the Sith. Despite an association with Palpatine of almost thirty years, Trachta saw the Sith as "arrogant fools," believing that the rule of the Sith kept all others from power. He intended to institute a secular organization, with himself as the head. Trachta's co-conspirators grew suspicious of his motives as the plot drew to a close, and the Grand Moff began using terms like "I" rather than "we" when speaking of his ambitions.<ref name="Betrayal 1" /> It would not be their suspicions that killed him, however, but the ambition of his own recruit, the young Moff Kadir. Trachta was considered dangerous enough that this was not anticipated to be an easy feat, but the Grand Moff was assassinated nonetheless.<ref name="RECG" />

===Grand Moff Bartam===
{{Main|Bartam}}
Along with Trachta, the corpulent Bartam was one of the highest-ranking Imperials to have a part in the conspiracy. In the final stages of the plot, Bartam recruited Gauer to his side, and the two men met multiple times, at one point discussing the idea of removing Trachta.<ref name="Betrayal 1" /> Nonetheless, though he disliked the other Grand Moff, Bartam had no actual intention of killing him. Trachta's suspicions of the possibility, however, led him to make the first move and have his fellow Grand Moff eliminated.<ref name="Fact File" />

===Gauer===
{{Main|Gauer}}
Gauer, the only member of the conspiracy to escape it alive, was an assassin in the employ of the Empire, and was even considered by Lord Vader to be capable of taking on a lightsaber-user in combat. Allying himself with Bartam and Kadir, Gauer eventually eliminated both men's rival in Trachta, and joined with Kadir for the final attack on the Emperor. As his allies and soldiers fell to Palpatine's guards and the Dark Lord himself, Gauer fled the scene.<ref name="Betrayal 3" />

===Moff Kadir===
{{Main|Kadir (Moff)}}
[[File:KadirPalpatine.jpg|thumb|right|170px|Kadir is punished by Palpatine with Force lightning.]]
Kadir, though a late addition to the conspiracy, eventually turned on its own architect and assumed control of it. Kadir was initially reprimanded by Trachta for his supposed innocence in matters of [[Politics/Legends|politics]], as the younger man advocated that all the conspirators trust each other. He later spoke with Bartam, stating a similar idea.<ref name="Betrayal 1" /> However, Kadir would prove to be less innocent than he appeared, as the ambitious Moff later joined forces with Gauer to have Trachta removed and take power on his own.<ref name="Fact File" /> After his men were killed, he even requested as high a position as the protégé of Palpatine himself; the Sith Lord, though impressed with his ingenuity, nonetheless forced him to watch his plot unravel, then killed him.<ref name="Betrayal 4" />

===General Skosef===
{{Main|Skosef}}
Skosef appeared to be an executor of the conspirators' will, assigned to tasks from planting bombs to carrying out assassinations. When the members of the cabal turned on each other, Skosef aligned himself with Trachta, and ultimately died carrying out the Grand Moff's assassination order.<ref name="Betrayal 3" />

==Behind the scenes==
The anti-Sith conspiracy first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Empire: Betrayal]]''. [[Abel G. Peña]], in writing the "Imperial Betrayal" article of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'', made mention of [[Atha Prime/Legends|Atha Prime]], a character originally created for a canceled line of toys by [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] and intended to be a "genetics master." It suggested that Prime and Trachta had been working together, and was intended to fuel speculation on the idea, first canonized in ''Betrayal'', that stormtroopers were clones. This reference, however, was cut from the final draft.<ref name="JCFCite">{{JCFCite|url=posts/26601966|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Abel G. Pena Discussion Thread|user=Halagad_Ventor|date=2007-04-22|time=8:11 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150908042203/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-abel-g-pena-discussion-thread.18750535/page-15}}</ref> Peña would later reference Trachta's collusion with Atha Prime by making him a possible agent of [[Zeta Magnus]] in his novella ''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''.<ref name="SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>

As explained by writer [[Scott Allie]], in the original drafts of ''[[Star Wars: Empire: Betrayal]]'' the other conspirators were not intended to be as ambitious as Trachta, and Kadir and Gauer did not usurp his role in the conspiracy.<ref name="Betrayal TPB">''[[Star Wars: Empire Volume 1: Betrayal]]''</ref>
